Welcome to Heady Docs

Everything you need to build with Heady Systems — from quick API calls to deep architecture dives. Whether you're integrating HeadyBuddy, deploying HeadyOS, or building on the API.

🚀

Quickstart

Get your first API call running in under 2 minutes.
⚡

API Reference

Complete endpoint documentation with examples.
💜

HeadyBuddy SDK

Build custom integrations with the Buddy SDK.
🖥️

HeadyOS Guide

Deploy and manage your AI operating system.

Quick API Example

Send your first message to HeadyBrain:

curl -X POST https://api.headysystems.com/api/brain/chat \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"message": "Hello Heady!"}'

Core Endpoints

Method Endpoint Description
POST /api/brain/chat Send a message to HeadyBrain AI
POST /api/vector/query Semantic search across vector memory
POST /api/vector/store Store content in vector memory
POST /api/memory/process Process and store a memory
GET /api/brain/health Check HeadyBrain health status
GET /api/vector/stats Get vector database statistics

Architecture Overview

20 Specialized AI Nodes

Heady operates as a distributed network of 20 specialized AI nodes, each responsible for a specific domain. Nodes communicate through the Conductor (orchestration layer), and all share a 3D vector memory space for persistent context.

Key Components

HeadyBrain API — Central intelligence gateway. Routes requests to optimal AI providers (Gemini, Claude, GPT, Groq) with automatic fallback.

3D Vector Memory — Persistent semantic memory using embedding-based storage. Facts, procedures, and context organized in 3D vector space.

HCFP Engine — Heady Core Functionality Platform. Auto-success execution engine that ensures zero-downtime operations.

Edge Proxy — Cloudflare Workers-based edge deployment for sub-50ms global latency.
